chapters.title: Reality isn't given but created
chapters.text:
Folks tend to fixate on the stuff they can touch instead of the unseen layer of life. Pam Grout enrolled in a self-paced course in spiritual psychology known as A Course in Miracles. There she picked up the idea that humans can preset what they will see or live through. Our minds scan the options and pick what gets noticed and believed. Whatever you fixate on becomes your experience. The version of reality you choose to observe is the only one that shapes your awareness. For instance, if you hold the world as thrilling, your attention lands on the bright parts of life. The reverse is also true.

Play out what you want to see until it becomes your reality.

Most people are loyal to things they don't want, since that is the world's default setting. So you should pick up a more positive way of thinking. Suppose you are broke. The first move is not chasing outside things that will fatten your wallet, like reading books and grinding harder. The real fix is to picture your new reality in your head. Start by thinking rich, value what you already have, and lack will fade on its own.

This summary walks through nine hands-on experiments you can try to grow your happiness. You will also learn how your mind builds your reality and how to wield words to shape the world around you.

chapters.title: The invisible force that can fulfill your aspirations
chapters.text:
At its core, the cosmos is an ocean of energy built from waves and particles. This field of potentiality, or FP, is the unseen force that shapes what we call reality. Unlike the solid objects we can poke and see, FP works backstage, molding the world in answer to our beliefs, expectations, and feelings. Yet, for all its muscle, FP stays underused, mostly because we were trained to trust only what we can see and measure.

Working with this energy field is a bit like tapping into electricity. It is dependable and within reach, but you need a clean connection. In other words, we have to spell out our intentions and treat our time with FP like any other reliable service. Be specific, be timely, and be confident.

To work with FP well, you have to drop the idea that something is broken in you, or that the cosmos is out to get you. Once you see the cosmos as supportive, you can move through life using these spiritual laws with confidence. It is about becoming so sure of your aims that the cosmos has to answer.

This way of life asks you to be bold, to set deadlines, and to truly believe that you have backup. Take this idea in, and watch unseen forces line up to build the world you want.

You can reach a field of potentiality any time once you're ready to focus on it.

The method

In this experiment, take two days to look around you for proof that FP exists. Drop doubt during that stretch and pick a good moment to start. Write the time and date in a notepad. Then ask for something. It could be a gift, a sign, a direction, or something that could never be a coincidence. Take some time to notice the presence of what you asked for. When you find it, you will know if FP is real or not.

chapters.title: Opportunities, like beauty, lie in the eye of the beholder
chapters.text:
Your life is a direct mirror of the thoughts and feelings you carry. Picture a young woman at the airport wrestling with three heavy bags. Her burden was not just physical. Her head was loaded with negativity. She kept grumbling about the bus that was supposed to pick her up. Without realizing it, the vehicle sat in plain sight, its doors wide open. The bus circled the airport twice, stopping for passengers, but the angry young woman could not see it. This tale shows what happens when complaints, fears, and resentment eat up all our attention.

Negative thoughts drain the effort and time you need to reach your targets.

When we adopt a certain mindset, it becomes the lens we use to see life. We learn to limit ourselves, hold a scarcity view, and compete with others. Yet we can also tell ourselves that we have a unique mission, inborn talents, and great skills to work with others. This way, we act out the visions we want or the views we want to take on.

The two-day test below will show you that your experiences mirror your pursuits. It will also prove that changing your focus can change the events in your life.

The method

For the next 48 hours, you will look for things around you on purpose. For the first 24 hours, pick any building and tell yourself that you will walk around looking for bungalows. On day two, plan to find green cars, yellow shirts, or anything else you choose. Look on purpose and write down how many of these things you see. There are things around you that you won't notice until you start looking for them.

Man's chief delusion is his belief that there are causes other than his own state of consciousness, as Neville Goddard once wrote.

chapters.title: You are an energy generator
chapters.text:
Your thoughts, feelings, and way of life shape the energy that flows through you, which is your personal vibration. When you feel excited, joyful, and grateful, those emotions broadcast a high-frequency signal that draws in more things to be thankful for. The reverse is also true. When you feel scared or weighed down by guilt, those emotions send out a low-frequency signal that pulls in ugly situations. This trial will show you that your thoughts produce real energetic impulses.

Build a list of things that lift you up and use it to raise your vibration.

The method

Set aside two hours for this experiment. Find something you can bend into a circle. Slice up some plastic straws and thread them onto the object before you bend it. Then rest the object on your chest and wait until the straws settle.

Once the straws have stopped moving, look straight ahead and call up every hard or painful memory from your past. Depending on how strong those memories feel, the object will either stay still or tip inward until the ends almost touch. The object follows your body's electromagnetic field, which has tightened because of the negative signal you have just produced.

Now picture something beautiful from your past, or something you would like to happen, and let the object open back out as the positive energy flows. Look straight ahead, fix your attention on that image, and watch the object respond to your thoughts.

chapters.title: Thoughts behave like boomerangs
chapters.text:
If you know what you want, you can have it. The opening move is to focus on that thing. But it is trickier than it sounds. Often, we hope for positive outcomes while quietly worrying about the chance of failure. As a result, your attitude can scatter the force in every direction.

The cosmos responds to every thought you have, so make sure positive ideas are the ones leading the way.

Sometimes it feels like our prayers go unanswered, but the truth is that we are praying for too many things that fight each other. Your thoughts carry real power. Do not waste your energy on small worries. Replace them with fresh ideas instead, and do not stress over what is going on in other people's heads. When insecurity creeps in, lean on your strengths and stop getting in your own way.

The method

Your thoughts are very powerful, and you can use them to pull things into your life. Choose something specific that you want to draw in. Be exact. Do not pick something unrealistic. For instance, if you want a laptop, name the brand and the model. Watch how the FP responds to your request.

Common sense is the collection of prejudices we pick up by age eighteen, as Albert Einstein once put it.

chapters.title: Guidance can show up anywhere
chapters.text:
When Jamie Lee was 25, she was at her home in Los Angeles with Debra Hill. She happened to see a magazine photo of three men, and one of them, Christopher Guest, a man she had never met, gave her a strong gut feeling. She was suddenly certain that they were meant to marry. By chance, Debra knew Guest's agent. So the very next day, Jamie called the agent, left her phone number, and asked that Guest give her a ring if he felt like it.

Guest never called. A few months later, Jamie ran into him at a restaurant, just three tables away. They waved at each other, and he moved on. The next day, Guest called Jamie and asked her out. They fell in love and were married within eight months of that first gut nudge.

Listening to your inner voice can spare you a lot of unnecessary stress.

Inner guidance is always available because the mind is wired to spot problems and aim for goals. That guidance can take many forms. It can be the reassuring voice you hear when you are worried, or the imaginary council meeting you run with a role model in your head. If you let yourself listen, you will often solve the problem or land on the answer you needed.

The method

This experiment will show you how to get quick guidance on a question. Pick something you really need answered within 48 hours. Choose an issue that calls for a yes or no answer, something that has you unsure and looking for direction. It could be whether to enroll in a school in your state, or as small as whether to wear a red outfit to a party. Look for a clear sign or hint within two days.

chapters.title: Words do far more than spell
chapters.text:
Masaru Emoto, a Japanese author, looked into how human words, thoughts, and feelings can shape physical things. He and his team worked with 10,000 water samples and exposed them to different types of music. When they put the ice crystals under a microscope, the ones from water they had spoken to with kind words like I love you came out clear and beautifully formed. Crystals from water they had cursed came out with dark, ugly holes. Emoto also pulled samples from a dam at Fujiwara Lake, where the crystals were dark and shapeless. After a monk led a prayer session over those samples, the once-distorted crystals turned into pristine, bright hexagonal shapes, with a smaller crystal nested inside. The lesson is that your words can shape the physical world.

Your words carry real power, so let them bring out the best in you and in others.

The method

Grab a green and a red container, some soil, and maize seeds. Fill each container with soil and plant two maize seeds in each. Put the containers near a window and water them every day. Each time you water them, say out loud that the maize in the red container will grow faster than the maize in the green one. Then write down what you observe over the week.

Worth noting: research by Robert Schrauf, an associate professor at Penn University, has found that the American vocabulary holds more words for negative emotions than for positive ones.

In short, this is one of those experiments where the message is hard to miss. Speak kindly and you help things grow. Speak harshly and you push them apart.

The takeaway is bigger than it looks. Once you start paying attention to what you say out loud and what you mutter under your breath, you realize that every phrase is a tiny seed, and over a week the difference shows up in how the plants lean toward the light.

chapters.title: Thoughts vs. health
chapters.text:
Our self-image, the way we talk to ourselves about our health, and our attitudes toward food are big factors in how healthy we are. The body is built to restore and keep its own balance, a process that happens on its own, without us thinking about it.

On the other hand, obsessing over calories can get in the way of reaching the body we want. That's because instead of enjoying the energy and joy that food gives us, we often treat meals with dislike and blame for not making us slimmer. Dwelling on negative body images and fixating on losing weight tends to backfire, bringing in more frustration.

The emotions you feel while eating also go into your body, so approach meals with gratitude.

The method

This trial asks you to drop the silent blame you have held against the food you eat. During this experiment, you will stop all kinds of negative talk about your body. You will send anything that goes into your body with loving thoughts and a blessing. Instead of telling everyone how fat and ugly you look, tell yourself that you are wonderful and have the perfect shape. Then shift your focus to filling your meals with love, joy, and calm. Note your weight when you start this new habit of positive words and check again after three days.

chapters.title: The ripple effect of your actions
chapters.text:
One man thought the United States' strategies were too aggressive and decided to stand against them. Each night, he would head out with a candle in hand, in a quiet protest. An onlooker once asked how such a small act could possibly change what the government did. The protester answered that his main goal was not to change the government. He did not want them to change him.

Everyone and everything around us is connected in such a way that your reactions toward others affect you, for better or worse. You can choose to be kind by thinking kind thoughts about others and the situations you find yourself in.

Every encounter happens for a reason, so treat it with respect and care.

Whenever we judge or think less of others, we hurt ourselves. We cause our own pain. When you meet someone, see it as a privilege and treat them the way you want to be treated. You can shift your relationship with someone by simply sending them good thoughts.

The method

The good thing about this experiment is that you do not even have to leave your chair. The steps are simple. Pick someone you know that you have friction with. Decide what response you would like to receive from this person. Be specific about what you want to see. Picture them right in front of you. Build a connection with the person in your mind. Bring in all your senses and trust the intent of your communication. Think good things about the person. Bless them and wish them well.

It is worth remembering that the experiment is really a small practice in forgiveness. Most of us carry a few grudges, and a single quiet session like this can loosen them.

chapters.title: Is scarcity even real?
chapters.text:
In an interview, a business leader whose thriving company was about to launch a new product was asked what financial success meant to him. He sighed and admitted that the money was never enough. That attitude is more common than you would think. Plenty of us, including people who are well off, live with the belief that there is a shortage of resources in the world. Scarcity and lack have become the default setting for many people. In fact, it has become part of our identity.

Subscribe to websites that share positive news and look at world events from a different angle.

Focusing on the negative keeps you far from reality. Instead of clinging to the old belief that nothing is ever enough, why not accept that you can live a rich and meaningful life? The point of the next experiment is to break apart the gloomy belief that life is nothing but a hard road. For the length of this experiment, set your usual pessimistic thoughts aside. Consider the hopeful possibility that there is enough out there for everyone.

The method

Over the next two days, keep track of good and beautiful things that happen around you. Carry a small journal with you for those two days. Write down every little detail. A kiss, a show of love and acceptance, or someone buying a meal for another person. Then watch how many good things are happening around us each day.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
We live in a world weighed down by many struggles, which makes being negative feel like the default. So many people have steered their lives with that mindset that it has dragged them into misery. The world may not look the way we want it to, but we can choose how we respond. We can choose to be positive, even in the middle of the toughest situations.

This summary covers nine experiments that show how our minds shape our lives. The first one aims to prove the existence of a higher power. Despite the many myths people carry about it, Pam Grout invites the reader to do some simple research. The second one shows that our thoughts and emotions are so powerful that they shape our lives. The third one proves that our thoughts and feelings create energy waves in our bodies.

The fourth reveals the power of focus. Focusing on what you want is the secret to making it real. The fifth talks about the power of guidance. It shows that there is an inner voice in everyone pointing us toward the right path. The sixth focuses on the power of words. Your life is shaped by what you say. So choose positive words for yourself and for the situations around you. The seventh is about not complaining about how you look, but saying what you want to see in your body. The eighth aims to build a positive relationship between yourself and everything around you. The last is about not living just to pile up wealth, but to live a meaningful and satisfying life.

Try this.

Gather a small group in your local area and share uplifting stories. Encourage each other. Create new experiments and watch the energy come alive in front of your eyes.

Did you read it all the way through?

The book is short, but its lessons are big. Take a few minutes after each chapter to write down what you want to try, and notice how the experiments change your week.

End-of-book note: a final reminder that every chapter leans on the same idea. What you pay attention to grows, and what you ignore fades. Pick the experiment that calls to you most, run it for a week, and write down what shifts.
